creator: Julie Bort
title: (No AI) Anthropic’s Claude is winning over paid consumers, a market owned by ChatGPT
dc:creator: Julie Bort
content: Despite ChatGPT's commanding market lead, consumers who pay for AI have been increasingly choosing Anthropic's Claude, data shows.
contentSnippet: Despite ChatGPT's commanding market lead, consumers who pay for AI have been increasingly choosing Anthropic's Claude, data shows.

creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) General Intuition’s $2.3B bet that video games can train AI agents for the real world
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: General Intuition has raised $320 million to scale AI trained on millions of hours of gameplay, betting action data can help AI develop something closer to human intuition.
contentSnippet: General Intuition has raised $320 million to scale AI trained on millions of hours of gameplay, betting action data can help AI develop something closer to human intuition.

title: (No AI) Ford had to hire back former engineers to fix mistakes made by its automated systems
author: Andrew J. Hawkins
content:
![The Ford logo on a graphic blue and white background.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/08/acastro_STK056_02.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

To celebrate its new status as No. 1 in JD Power's initial quality ranking among mainstream automakers, Ford is opening up about the challenges it has faced in recent years, especially around its reliance on automated systems in production and design. It turns out that those automated systems were not as robust as previously assumed, requiring Ford to hire experienced technicians - sometimes bringing back former employees - to correct errors made by the company's robots.

In Ford's view, AI is both powerful and prone to pitfalls. Its effectiveness depends entirely on the quality of the data used to train the AI models. In addition, the auto …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/transportation/956316/ford-quality-jd-power-ranking-ai-automated-mistakes)

To celebrate its new status as No. 1 in JD Power's initial quality ranking among mainstream automakers, Ford is opening up about the challenges it has faced in recent years, especially around its reliance on automated systems in production and design. It turns out that those automated systems were not as robust as previously assumed, requiring Ford to hire experienced technicians - sometimes bringing back former employees - to correct errors made by the company's robots. 
In Ford's view, AI is both powerful and prone to pitfalls. Its effectiveness depends entirely on the quality of the data used to train the AI models. In addition, the auto …
Read the full story at The Verge.

It turns out that those automated systems were not as robust as previously assumed, […]

title: (No AI) Facebook’s Creator Studio has been revived as an AI companion app
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![Meta’s new Creator Studio app for Facebook creators.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/Facebook-Creator-Studio-relaunch.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

This is what the new Creator Studio app looks like, after the original service was shuttered in 2023. | Image: Meta

Meta is bringing back the [Facebook Creator Studio](https://creators.facebook.com/blog/introducing-new-facebook-creator-studio) page manager, now "reimagined" as a standalone AI companion app. The new app aims to make it easier for creators to connect with their audiences and show them "exactly how to grow on Facebook," according to Meta's announcement.

[Meta's AI Creator Assistant](https://about.fb.com/news/2026/06/creator-assistant-more-languages-for-ai-translations-on-facebook/) is a central focus of the newly reimagined app - users can ask the chatbot to provide performance tracking insights and tailored recommendations for improving engagement. It can also be used to find "the most important comments" left by the user's audience and "instantly draft replies in your voice."

![Examples of AI-powered commenting features inside the reimagined Facebook Creator Studio.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/Screenshot-2026-06-25-095344.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100 "Examples of AI-powered commenting features inside the reimagined Facebook Creator Studio.")

The Creator Studio app isn't widely ava …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/956668/meta-facebook-creator-studio-ai-app-relaunch)

title: (No AI) How agents are transforming work
content: A new OpenAI research paper shows how AI agents are transforming work, enabling longer, more complex tasks and expanding productivity across roles.
contentSnippet: A new OpenAI research paper shows how AI agents are transforming work, enabling longer, more complex tasks and expanding productivity across roles.

title: (No AI) OpenAI and Broadcom unveil LLM-optimized inference chip
content: OpenAI and Broadcom introduce Jalapeño, a custom AI chip built for LLM inference to improve performance, efficiency, and scale across AI systems.
contentSnippet: OpenAI and Broadcom introduce Jalapeño, a custom AI chip built for LLM inference to improve performance, efficiency, and scale across AI systems.

creator: Sam Cunningham
title: (No AI) World Cup Teams Are in a Race for AI Dominance
dc:creator: Sam Cunningham
content: This year, FIFA is providing an AI agent that any team can use. Is it enough to level the playing field or will future winners be determined by which team can afford the best tools?
contentSnippet: This year, FIFA is providing an AI agent that any team can use. Is it enough to level the playing field or will future winners be determined by which team can afford the best tools?

creator: Matt Burgess, Mark Wilding
title: (No AI) British Police Built a Sprawling Crime-Prediction Machine. Some Results Couldn’t Be Trusted
dc:creator: Matt Burgess, Mark Wilding
content: As UK police embrace the AI revolution, a WIRED investigation reveals the messy inside story of one region’s experiment with predictive analytics.
contentSnippet: As UK police embrace the AI revolution, a WIRED investigation reveals the messy inside story of one region’s experiment with predictive analytics.
